pages.raw_text:
After having made charges
against Officer Miller, who had
earned her disfavor by participa-
ton in the raid on the Miners
hotel, Sprague and Stevens, in
whihc she was arrested yesterday
morning, Agnes Morgan, a fiery
tempered hair dresser, signed aft
davits that her accusations on the
stand were false. This was done
in police court yesterday AFTER
noon, where she was arraigned be-
fore Judge Mann. together with
Ethel Brown, May Hays, Della and
Lewis Marshall, Violet Kelley and
Charles Anderson.


Both the Morgan woman and
Ethel Brown were charged with
vagrancy when brought in by the
squad of officers, but as she
proved to the court she made her
living in a hair dressing parlor,
that charge was dismissed. She
was Immediately arraigned for dis
orderly conduct and was convicted
and was fined s25 and costs. Ethel
Brown was tried for the same Of
Tense and fined $40 and costs.


The cases of Charles Anderson,
proprietor of the hotel, accused of
running a disorderly house, and
with vagrancy, were continued till
next week. Della is held under
$50 bond and Lewis 500. May
Hayes and Violet Kelley, convicted
of vagrancy, We're each fined $50
and trimmings. Marjorie Schultz
forfeited her bond of $25 by her
failure to appear. Agnes Morgon
was lucky to escape trial for per-
jury and would have been called
on to substantiate her charges had
she not acknowledged their I'M
truth.
